text: The surgeon begins the transverse colectomy by opening the peritoneum over the middle colic vessels, marking a ten-centimeter margin along the bowel wall. A window is created to the right of the middle colics, exposing the middle colic artery and vein. The surgeon traces the anterior surface of the superior mesenteric vein (SMV) and opens over the middle colic pedicle, creating a window around it. The dissection proceeds close to the base of the pancreas and stomach, following the mesentery to the distal colon margin. The omentum is divided at the planned proximal and distal margins, and the surgeon enters the lesser sac through the gastrocolic omentum to detach residual attachments to the transverse colon.

The surgeon addresses tension at the colon margins due to flexures, removing adhesions between the hepatic flexure and gallbladder. Indocyanine green (ICG) is used to assess perfusion, revealing a more proximal bowel margin than initially cauterized. The specimen is placed in the pelvis, and the surgeon lines up the two ends of the colon with 3-0 silk for a sewn end-to-end anastomosis.

The surgeon begins the anastomosis by sewing the two staple lines together using a two OPDS suture, tying it to the threostase suture. Colotomies are made, and ICG is checked to confirm perfusion before suturing the common channel closed, starting from the far corner.

I think for most of us who still feel that an extended right or left is the better oncologic operation for a transverse colon cancer need to accept that that's not necessarily correct. It's been shown over and over again in multiple meta-analyses. that the oncologic outcome with even what you see me do here is more than adequate. In this case, there were twenty-seven lymph nodes that were all negative and I feel like I've offered the patient an adequate oncologic surgery. I think the benefit of keeping the entire right colon because otherwise this would have been an extended right for me is paramount to an older patient where bowel function can be compromised. I end up extracting my specimen through a small fan steel incision This patient did very well. This was a morning of postoperative day one discharge.
The surgeon brings two sutures towards the middle to tie them together, completing the anastomosis. The final anterior outer layer is sewn using PDS sutures.
